quoteFirst: Mademoiselle Mimi, who was accustomed to sleep far into the day, woke up one morning
quoteLast: , and was greatly surprised not to find Rodolphe beside her, nor even in the room. The preceding night, before falling to sleep, she had, however, seen him at his desk, preparing to spend the night over a piece of literary work which had been ordered of him, and in the completion of which Mimi was especially interested.
title: Bohemians of the Latin Quarter
author: Murger, Henri, 1822-1861

quoteFirst: How long this would have lasted I don't know, for old Chuck was game to the death and had throwed away his coat, vest, hat and boots and was bound to catch them stock cars, and the switchman and engineer was bound he shouldn't. But finally the engine had to stop for coal and water, and they shoved us in on a sidetrack, went off to bed and left us there till
quoteLast: the next day. But I never shall forget the anguish and horror we endured for fear we wouldn't find that way-car and they would pull the stock out and leave us there.
title:
Cowboy Life on the Sidetrack
Being an Extremely Humorous & Sarcastic Story of the Trials & Tribulations Endured by a Party of Stockmen Making a Shipment from the West to the East.
author: Benton, Frank, 1853-1921; Filleau, E. A. [Illustrator]

quoteFirst: "Then we shall be in camp by
quoteLast: . I don't see the sense of yanking a fellow out of bed in the night."
title: Captured by the Navajos
author: Curtis, Charles A. (Charles Albert), 1835-1907

quoteFirst: “There are many thanks to be offered to you, and to be transmitted to you. Be this evening about
quoteLast: at St. Cloud, in front of the pavilion which stands at the corner of the house of M. d’Estrées.—C.B.”
title: The three musketeers
author: Dumas, Alexandre, 1802-1870; Maquet, Auguste, 1813-1888

quoteLast: P. M. Felicia, the maid, left by the basement door with the policeman to get a raspberry phosphate around the corner. She detested the policeman and objected earnestly to the arrangement.
title: Whirligigs
author: Henry, O., 1862-1910

quoteFirst: A revolution had been attempted, and the leaders of the conspiracy, Count Brahe and Baron Horn, were executed on that day. Swedenborg writes of Brahe thus:—“Brahe was beheaded
quoteLast: in the morning, and spoke with me at ten at night; that is to say, twelve hours after his execution. He was with me almost without interruption for several days.
title:
Life of Emanuel Swedenborg
Together with a brief synopsis of his writings, both philosophical and theological
author: White, William, 1832?-1890

quoteFirst: On the other side, once an old garden, there is now a long, rambling wall, with here and there an opening through which, to your surprise, you catch the drooping figure of a poor, forlorn mule, condemned for some crime of his ancestors to go round and round in a treadmill, grinding refuse brick. Along the quay or _fondamenta_ of this narrow canal, always shady after
quoteLast: , lie sprawled the younger members of these tenements--the children, bareheaded, barefooted, and most of them barebacked; while their mothers and sisters choke up the doorways, stringing beads, making lace, sitting in bunches listening to a story by some old crone, or breaking out into song, the whole neighborhood joining in the chorus.
title: Gondola days
author: Smith, Francis Hopkinson, 1838-1915

quoteLast: when Emil Einstein sprang down the stairway of the eastern terminus of the Brooklyn Bridge. The lad was blithe at heart as he turned to the left and, passing through the seething press of the crowds congested under the electric lights of Sands and Fulton Streets, carefully reconnoitered a gorgeous saloon on the corner of Layte and Dale Streets.
title: The Midnight Passenger : A Novel
author: Savage, Richard, 1846-1903

quoteLast: before the stew was ready to be dished up. The aroma of it awakened the hungry men.
title: Navy boys behind the big guns
author: Davidson, Halsey; Owen, Robert Emmett, 1878-1957 [Illustrator]
